title: Marine Superintendent
description:
Job Description

  • Attend vessels to verify compliance with health, safety and environmental protection policy and standards onboard
  • Guide vessels to ensure compliance with company policies, international rules and regulations, and oil major’s requirements
  • Advise on changes in legislation and/or industry best practices and submit recommendations for approval
  • Propose amendments to the company’s SMS manuals for approval
  • Conduct annual internal Safety Inspections (Marine) and Navigational audits onboard vessels
  • Conduct internal ISM audits and coordinate external ISM/ISPS/MLC audits onboard vessels
  • Guide vessels in preparation for SIRE inspections and carry out pre-vetting inspections onboard prior to formal inspection
  • Arrange and attend SIRE inspections and follow up with rectification reports within due dates for approval
  • Follow up on vessels' PSC/FSC inspections and other external inspections/surveys (terminal inspections, P&I surveys, banker inspections, etc.)
  • Conduct investigations and report for marine incidents, accidents, casualties, and customer complaints, as required
  • Implement and monitor corrective actions for any non-conformities related to health, safety and environmental protection
  • Evaluate Masters' and sea staff’s performance through onboard internal inspections and propose appropriate actions
  • Conduct crew interviews as required
  • Prepare and upload relevant TMSA elements as assigned
  • Monitor and process vessels' routine reports
  • Monitor vessels' deficiency reports, especially for critical equipment, to ensure timely close-out
  • Carry out sale and purchase inspections and tasks related to vessel take-over and newbuild projects, as assigned
  • Arrange vessel safety equipment servicing (life rafts, fire-fighting appliances, etc.) within due dates
  • Monitor and supply vessel operations related equipment servicing (cargo hoses, fenders, mooring ropes, UTI, etc.)
  • Verify quotations/invoices for deck repairs, stores, maintenance and servicing for approval
  • Control budgets for deck stores, spares and repairs
  • Any other duties as assigned by the Marine Manager


Job Requirement

  • COC Class 1 or 2 holders with sailing experience on oil tankers
  • At least 1 years’ experience as a Marine Superintendent in ship management operations
  • Foreign-going chief officer with sea experience preferred
  • Strong communication and interpersonal skills
  • Ability to handle multiple projects and good organization skills
  • Analytical, able to work independently, results-oriented, resourceful, and pro-active

postedCompany.description:
Vitol is an energy and commodities company; its primary business is the trading and distribution of energy products globally – it trades over seven million barrels per day of crude oil and products and, at any time, has 250 ships transporting its cargoes.

Vitol’s clients include national oil companies, multinationals, leading industrial and chemical companies and the world’s largest airlines. Founded in Rotterdam in 1966, today Vitol serves clients from some 40 offices worldwide and is invested in energy assets globally including: circa 18mm3 of storage across seven continents, 480kbpd of refining capacity and 5,000 service stations across Africa, Australia, Eurasia and in Northwest Europe. Revenues in 2018 were in excess of $200bn. For more information: www.vitol.com


VBunkers is a 100% subsidiary of the Vitol Group, an expert bunker tanker manager and operator with extensive experience of physical bunker supplies in the port of Singapore, the world’s largest bunkering port, and a fleet of 15 modern Singapore flagged bunker tankers, including Singapore’s first and currently only Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) powered dual fuel bunker tanker “Marine Vicky”.
postedCompany.name: V-BUNKERS TANKERS PTE. LTD.

districts.location: D05 Clementi New Town, Hong Leong Garden, Pasir Panjang
